answer: On September 15, 2026 the settlement_disbursement_recon_v3 pipeline was deployed with a bad join key (normalized_tax_id) when joining settlement.escrow_disbursement_fact to party.party_identity_bridge. Because normalized_tax_id matched multiple buyer and seller party roles within the same closing, escrow disbursements were duplicated in finance.disbursement_recon_daily, overstating the escrow disbursement reconciliation variance for Florida purchase closings under FNF National Title. The fix is to switch the join to closing_party_role_id, quarantine normalized_tax_id joins in reconciliation workloads, and backfill the affected Florida data.

facts.affected_segment: Florida purchase closings for FNF National Title
facts.remediation: Switch the transform to closing_party_role_id, quarantine normalized_tax_id joins in reconciliation workloads, and backfill Florida purchase closings for FNF National Title
facts.root_cause: settlement_disbursement_recon_v3 joined party_identity_bridge by normalized_tax_id, which matched multiple buyer and seller party roles inside the same closing, duplicating escrow disbursements in finance.disbursement_recon_daily
